message.content:
I need to understand two things:

1. **Mobile app's main/home page** — Find and explore `apps/mobile/src/screens/Home/HomeFeedScreen.tsx` (or similar). I need to understand:
   - The full layout/JSX structure (header, search bar presence, feed area)
   - What components are used in the header area
   - Whether there's already a search bar or SearchInput component on this screen
   - The navigation flow — does tapping search navigate somewhere else (like ExploreScreen)?

2. **Web app's layout with search bar** — Look in `iskrib_v3/` or similar web app directory for the main page/layout that has a search bar. I need to understand:
   - Where the search bar is positioned in the web app's main page
   - What the search bar does (inline search? navigates to search page?)
   - The visual layout pattern (search bar in header? below header? sidebar?)

3. **Existing SearchInput component** — Find the mobile app's SearchInput component and understand its API/props.

4. **ExploreScreen** — Check `apps/mobile/src/screens/Home/ExploreScreen.tsx` to understand how search currently works on the Explore tab.

Be thorough — read the full JSX return of HomeFeedScreen and the web app's main layout component.

content.text: I'll explore all four areas in parallel to give you a comprehensive understanding.

content.text:
I now have everything needed. Here is the full, comprehensive analysis: --- ## 1. HomeFeedScreen — Full Layout Analysis **File:** `C:/Users/johnm/iskrib_mobile_app/apps/mobile/src/screens/Home/HomeFeedScreen.tsx` ### JSX Structure (top to bottom) ``` {/* HEADER — branded title only */} ← paddingHorizontal: lg, paddingVertical: md, hairline bottom border ← "iskrib", fontFamily: fonts.brand.semiBold, fontSize: 24, letterSpacing: 1 iskrib {/* FILTER CHIPS — horizontal scroll */} ← paddingVertical: sm {/* FEED — infinite scroll FlatList */} ItemSeparatorComponent → /> {/* MODALS (conditional) */} ← only when commentModal state is set ← only when repostModal state is set {/* FAB — Write button */} ← amber circle, 56x56, borderRadius 28 ``` ### Key findings - **No search bar exists on HomeFeedScreen at all.** The header is just a branded "iskrib" wordmark text. No `SearchInput`, no search icon, no pressable search field. - **No navigation to ExploreScreen from the header.** The only navigation calls in this file are `PostDetail`, `VisitProfile`, `JournalEditor (create)`, and a tab switch to `Profile`. - **Chip filter** only toggles a local `activeFilter` state (`'all'` vs `'journal'`), but notably this filter is not actually applied to the FlatList `data` — the `posts` array is derived purely from the feed query regardless of `activeFilter`. The filter chips are visual-only at this point. - **FAB** navigates to `JournalEditor` with `mode: 'create'`. --- ## 2. SearchInput Component **File:** `C:/Users/johnm/iskrib_mobile_app/apps/mobile/src/components/SearchInput.tsx` ### Props API ```typescript interface SearchInputProps extends Omit { containerStyle?: any; // optional override for the outer View style } // All other TextInput props (value, onChangeText, placeholder, autoCapitalize, // returnKeyType, onFocus, onBlur, etc.) pass through via spread ``` ### Visual Structure ``` ← left-leading icon, theme-aware ``` ### Key facts - It is a **fully controlled, inline search input** — it does NOT navigate anywhere on its own. - It does not expose a right-side clear button or any adornment beyond the leading SearchIcon. - `containerStyle` allows overriding the outer container's positioning/sizing without touching the internal text input style. - Height is fixed at **44px**. --- ## 3. ExploreScreen — How Search Currently Works **File:** `C:/Users/johnm/iskrib_mobile_app/apps/mobile/src/screens/Home/ExploreScreen.tsx` ### Layout structure (return JSX) ``` ← flex: 1 {/* HEADER BLOCK */} ← paddingHorizontal: lg, paddingTop: md, gap: md Explore {isSearching ? renderSearchView() : renderDefaultView()} ``` ### Search flow - `query` state is live-bound to `SearchInput`'s `value`/`onChangeText`. - `isSearching` = `normalizedQuery.trim().length >= 2` — this is the gate for showing results. - **Two queries** fire when `isSearching`: - `mobileApi.searchUsers(normalizedQuery, 10)` — if tab is not `'posts'` - `mobileApi.searchJournals(normalizedQuery, 10)` — if tab is not `'users'` - Default view (no search query): shows **hottest monthly posts** with a hero card + ranked runners. - Search view (`isSearching === true`): shows tab chips (`All / Users / Posts`), then users list (avatar rows) and/or PostCards. - **No navigation occurs** — search is entirely inline on the Explore tab. The SearchInput and results all live on the same screen. - There is **no debounce** — queries fire on every keystroke once length >= 2. --- ## 4. Web App's Main Page & Search Layout **File:** `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/HomePage/Home.jsx` ### Overall page layout The web app uses a **three-column layout** (`home-parent-container`): ```
← LEFT column ← vertical nav sidebar
← CENTER column (main content) ← React Router nested route
← RIGHT column ← opinions/trending sidebar
← floating action button (hidden on /home and /home/opinions) ← bottom nav for mobile ``` The **Sidebar** (left column) contains: the "Iskrib" wordmark → nav links (Home, Explore, Gallery, Profile, Notifications, Bookmarks, Settings, Write) → dark/light mode toggle → user avatar + name + sign out. **No search bar anywhere in the Sidebar.** The **center column** is a React Router `` — whatever nested route is active renders here. The home feed (`/home`) renders PostCards. The explore page (`/home/explore`) renders the ExplorePage component. ### Web app's Explore search — `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/HomePage/explore/ExplorePage.jsx` The search lives inside the ExplorePage component, which is the **Explore route's center column content**. Layout: ```
{/* SEARCH SHELL — at top of center column */}
← Search magnifier icon
{searchInput ? : null} Search | Matched | Searching...
{/* AUTOCOMPLETE DROPDOWN — appears while focused + 2+ chars */} {showSuggestions && (
{/* suggestion items or user cards */}
)} {/* PEOPLE / POSTS TOGGLE — appears when search is active */} {(isSearchFocused || searchInput || isSearchMode) && (
)}
{/* CONTENT — section title then hero card + runners OR search results */}
Hottest posts | Search results | People
...
``` ### Web search behavior — key differences from mobile | Aspect | Web (ExplorePage) | Mobile (ExploreScreen) | |---|---|---| | Search trigger | Enter key commits; dropdown auto-suggests on debounce (350ms) | Live query fires on every keystroke >= 2 chars | | Autocomplete | Yes — dropdown shows up to 6 suggestions as you type | No autocomplete dropdown | | People/Posts toggle | Appears when search is active (Posts / People buttons) | Tab chips shown after search activates (All / Users / Posts) | | People search data source | `searchUsers` API | `mobileApi.searchUsers` | | Clear button | Explicit "Clear" button appears when input has text | No clear button (only backspace) | | Navigation on result click | `navigate('/home/post/{id}')` | `navigation.navigate('PostDetail', {journalId})` | | Search position | Top of the center content column (not in sidebar/header) | Inside the screen header block below "Explore" title | --- ## Summary of What You Need to Know **HomeFeedScreen has no search bar, no search state, and no navigation to Explore triggered by search.** It is purely a branded header + filter chips + infinite feed + FAB. **SearchInput** is a simple, fully-controlled, theme-aware input with a leading SearchIcon, height 44, that passes all `TextInputProps` through — ready to be dropped in anywhere. **ExploreScreen owns all search logic inline.** Tapping the Explore tab takes you there; the SearchInput is already mounted in its header. There is no "tap to navigate to search" pattern — search is persistent on that screen. **Web app positions search at the top of the center column on the Explore route** (not in the global sidebar/header), with an autocomplete dropdown and a debounced commit-on-Enter model — more sophisticated than the current mobile implementation.
